title: festuca ovina subsp. liviensis
intro: Festuca ovina subsp. liviensis, also known as sheep's fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to the mountains of northern Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds and reduce pain. The health benefits of Festuca ovina subsp. liviensis include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve respiratory health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
